g.data-narration: Design Thinking is a human-centered creative problem-solving approach that helps teams innovate by deeply understanding users and their needs across five iterative stages.
Stage 1 is Empathize. You understand users deeply through observation, interviews, and immersion to uncover their real needs and pain points.
g.text: Understand users deeply: observation, interviews, field studies.
Tools: User personas, empathy maps, journey mapping.

g.data-narration: Stage 2 is Define. You synthesize research findings into a clear problem statement using POV framing and How Might We questions.
g.text: Synthesize insights into clear problem statements.
POV: User + Need + Insight | HMW: How Might We questions.

g.data-narration: Stage 3 is Ideate. This creative phase involves generating a wide variety of ideas through brainstorming, SCAMPER method, worst possible idea exercises, and mind mapping techniques without any judgment or criticism.
g.text: Methods: Brainstorming, SCAMPER, worst possible idea, mind maps.

g.data-narration: Stage 4 is Prototype. This hands-on phase involves building quick, inexpensive representations of ideas using paper, cardboard, wireframes, or mock-ups to transform abstract concepts into testable physical or digital forms.
g.text: Create low-fidelity models to test ideas quickly.
Types: Paper prototypes, wireframes, mock-ups, role-playing.

g.data-narration: Stage 5 is Test. Gather user feedback through usability studies and A/B testing to refine and iterate on your solution.
g.text: Gather user feedback to refine solutions.
Methods: Usability studies, A/B testing, user interviews.

g.data-narration: Design Thinking embraces three key principles: bias toward action helps teams build prototypes instead of overthinking, radical collaboration brings diverse perspectives together, and failing forward transforms mistakes into valuable learning opportunities for continuous improvement.
g.text: • Bias toward Action: Build prototypes over lengthy analysis.
• Fail Forward: Treat failures as learning opportunities.
